steps.title: Spot the generalization problem
steps.content: Researchers studied customers in Serbia (a developing economy) but cautioned against applying findings to customers generally. The warning signals that a finding from one economic context cannot be assumed to apply broadly.

steps.title: Match the answer to the concern
steps.content: B explains the solution: since the sample represents only one economic context, additional research with participants from countries of varying economic development is needed to determine if the pattern holds broadly. A inappropriately applies the finding to other countries, C addresses replication within Serbia, and D speculates without support. That matches choice B — B is correct.
